---

## Mission

We believe that open source software is the future — not just for developers, but for everyone. Our mission is to make that future **accessible**, **approachable**, and **empowering**.

We build software, products, communities, and coalitions that bridge the gap between the open source world and the everyday person. From operating systems to developer tools, everything we create is designed to be **intuitive**, **beautiful**, and **free**.

<!-- PROJECTS -->
## What We Build

| Project | Description | Status |
| :------ | :---------- | :----- |
| [CryFox](https://github.com/arcbasehq/cryfox) | A private, secure, and fast Firefox fork | 🟢 |
| [Sentinel](https://github.com/arcbasehq/sentinel) | Open source security for the modern web | 🟢 |
| [ArcOSINT](https://github.com/arcbasehq/arcsecurity) | Advanced OSINT in a modern terminal UI | 🟢 |
| [Arc Manager](https://github.com/arcbasehq/arcmanager) | The ultimate Linux package manager frontend | 🟢 |
| [ArcOS](https://github.com/arcbasehq/arcos) | Windows optimized for developers | 🟢 |
| [OpenShelf](https://github.com/arcbasehq/openshelf) | Open source library cataloging tool | 🟢 |
| [Arcbase Learning](https://github.com/arcbasehq/arcbase-app) | Master coding on Android | 🔶 |

---

## Principles

- **Openness First** — Transparency is our foundation
- **Community Driven** — Our users shape what we build  
- **Design Matters** — Open source shouldn't mean ugly
- **Innovation Forward** — We challenge the status quo

<!-- CONTRIBUTING -->
## Contributing

First off, thank you for considering contributing to arcbase! We are a community-driven organization building open source tools for a freer, more secure web.

### How Can I Contribute?

#### Bug Reports
Search existing issues first. If new, include:
- Descriptive title
- Steps to reproduce
- Expected vs actual behavior
- Environment details

> ⚠️ **Security Vulnerabilities**: Email [arcbase@tuta.io](mailto:arcbase@tuta.io) — **DO NOT** report via public issues.

#### Feature Requests
1. Check if already proposed
2. Open an issue describing the "Why" and "How"
3. Explain the problem it solves

#### Code Contributions
1. Find an issue with `good first issue` or `help wanted`
2. Fork & clone the repo
3. Create a branch (e.g., `feature/your-feature`)
4. Write code and tests
5. Open a PR with clear description

### Development Ecosystem

- **Web**: Astro, TypeScript, Vanilla CSS
- **Core Systems**: Go, Rust, C++
- **Security Tools**: Python, Go

---

<!-- ISSUE TEMPLATES -->
## Issue Templates

When opening an issue, use one of these formats:

### Bug Report
```markdown
## Bug Description
<!-- Describe the bug clearly -->

## Version
<!-- e.g., v1.2.0 -->

## Steps to Reproduce
1. 
2. 
3.

## Expected Behavior
<!-- What should happen -->

## Actual Behavior
<!-- What actually happens -->

## Environment
- OS:
- Browser:
- Version:
```

### Feature Request
```markdown
## Summary
<!-- Short description -->

## Problem/Motivation
<!-- What problem does this solve? -->

## Proposed Solution
<!-- Your idea -->

## Alternatives
<!-- Other solutions considered -->
```

---

<!-- PULL REQUEST TEMPLATE -->
## Pull Request Template

```markdown
## Description
<!-- Describe your changes -->

## Related Issue
<!-- Link to issue (e.g., Closes #123) -->

## Type of Change
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Documentation

## Checklist
- [ ] Code follows style guidelines
- [ ] Self-review done
- [ ] Tests added/pass
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] No new warnings
```

---

<!-- SUPPORT -->
## Support

### Getting Help
- Check project README and docs
- Open a GitHub issue for questions

### Security Issues
**Email**: [arcbase@tuta.io](mailto:arcbase@tuta.io)

Include: description, steps to reproduce, potential impact, fixes (if known).

We aim to acknowledge within 48 hours.

---

<!-- SECURITY -->
## Security Policy

### Supported Versions

| Version | Supported |
| ------- | ---------- |
| 1.x.x   | ✅ |

### Reporting

> **Do NOT report via public issues.**

Email: [arcbase@tuta.io](mailto:arcbase@tuta.io)

Response timeline: 48 hours acknowledge, coordinated disclosure after fix.

### Best Practices
- Never commit secrets/keys
- Follow least privilege
- Keep dependencies updated
